orgPolicy.title: New Broker License Verbiage in Offer Letters
orgPolicy.summary: Updated verbiage for the 'Does this role require a broker license?' offer question for CX & Sales roles.
bullets: When 'Yes' is selected for broker license question, updated verbiage now populates in the offer letter.
New language covers Licensing Requirement, timeline (3 months), and Licensing Costs (Gusto reimburses approved costs).
Applies to CX & Sales roles only — no change needed for other orgs.

tp.point: Offer letter verbiage for broker license roles has been updated — double-check your offer details before sending.

orgPolicy.title: Updated Relocation Document for California
orgPolicy.summary: Effective June 18: new dropdown field in offer details to identify California-bound relocations, ensuring correct legal documents are generated.
bullets: New dropdown: 'Is the relocation to California?' — select Yes or No.
California requires specific clawback language in relocation repayment agreements.
The correct documents will generate automatically based on your selection.
This went live June 18 — ensure all new relocation offers use the updated field.

tp.audience: All recruiters with candidates opting into relocation
tp.point: New California relocation field is live — always select Yes or No when a candidate is relocating. Selecting the wrong option generates the wrong legal documents.

orgPolicy.title: Compensation Bands Now in Workday (GSheets Deprecated)
orgPolicy.summary: The comp band GSheets are no longer functioning. Workday now has a live report for salary ranges and compensation bands.
bullets: Search by Job Family to get all ranges across levels and comp zones.
Access: https://wd5.myworkday.com/gusto/d/inst/1422$5089/rel-task/3001$14.htmld — bookmark it.
All Job Requisitions in Workday now have comp bands assigned; data is also feeding into Greenhouse & PeopleOS.
New Hire grant information still in development; expected to be in place shortly.

tp.point: Stop using comp band GSheets — they no longer work. Use the live Workday report instead. Share the bookmark link with your team.

orgPolicy.title: Process Changes Require Early Flagging to IOPE
orgPolicy.summary: Increase in urgent system change requests noted. All process changes that impact Greenhouse configuration must be flagged early.
bullets: Process changes include: custom field updates (offer detail/job info), verbiage changes, any system configuration.
Submit a ticket via go/phc if unsure whether a change has system impact.
Avoid rush changes that bypass validation/testing.

tp.point: If you're aware of any upcoming process changes that touch Greenhouse, flag them to IOPE early — don't wait until it's urgent.

orgPolicy.title: Hiring Committee: Invite Leadership Now Facilitating
orgPolicy.summary: Invite Leadership (PEs) are now facilitating HC sessions and follow-ups. Invite Ops is still supporting with candidate preps.
bullets: For questions or updates on submitted candidates, reach out to your Invite PE — not Invite Ops.
Invite Ops continues to handle candidate prep support.

tp.point: HC ownership has shifted — Invite PEs are now running HC sessions and follow-ups. Direct candidate status questions to your PE, not Invite Ops.

orgPolicy.title: Variable Cash Award Field in Greenhouse Offer Details
orgPolicy.summary: New question added to GH offer details: 'Are they receiving a % Variable Cash Award?' with three options.
bullets: No - Hourly/Not Eligible: no VC verbiage in offer
Yes - US Candidate: populates US-based VC verbiage
Yes - CANADA Candidate: populates Canada-based VC verbiage
Variable targets range 5–10% depending on level (L1-L3: 5%, L4: 7.5%, L5: 10%); 50% individual / 50% company split.
Double-check offer details before sending — accuracy is critical.

tp.point: New Variable Cash Award field is live in Greenhouse. You must select the correct option for every offer — it determines which VC verbiage appears in the offer letter.

items.heading: Auto-Population of Offer Details — In Progress
bullets: Invite Ops + PeopleTech are working to auto-populate job-related fields in offer details to reduce manual effort.
More details coming soon.

items.heading: IKB Migration to Notion — Ongoing
bullets: Ops continues migrating Invite Knowledge Base (IKB) resources into Notion.
Goal: consolidated, simpler navigation for all IKB resources.
